English: Abdullahi Yasin Warsame, 42, told Radio Ergo that his household of 13 people relied entirely on food distributions and a small cash stipend that suddenly stopped in February.
Kiswahili: Abdullahi Yasin Warsame, 42, aliiambia Radio Ergo kwamba kaya yake ya watu 13 ilitegemea kabisa usambazaji wa chakula na posho ndogo ya pesa ambayo ilikoma ghafla mnamo Februari.

English: He and his family were removed from the eligibility list after being classified by the authorities as having Kenyan citizenship.
Kiswahili: Yeye na familia yake waliondolewa kwenye orodha ya wanaostahiki baada ya kuainishwa na mamlaka kuwa na uraia wa Kenya.

English: Abdullahi denies this, saying neither he, his wife, nor his mother are Kenyan citizens, and that his children are all still minors.
Kiswahili: Abdullahi anakanusha hili, akisema yeye, mke wake, wala mama yake si raia wa Kenya, na kwamba watoto wake wote bado ni watoto.

English: He said he has not received a clear explanation, despite submitting many complaints to authorities and aid agency offices.
Kiswahili: Alisema hajapata maelezo ya kueleweka, licha ya kuwasilisha malalamiko mengi kwa mamlaka na ofisi za mashirika ya misaada.

English: When you lose what you had, you realise what it meant to you.
Kiswahili: Unapopoteza ulichokuwa nacho, unatambua maana yake kwako.

English: We had no other source of income.
Kiswahili: Hatukuwa na chanzo kingine cha mapato.

English: When it was taken away and we didn’t know why, it caused us a lot of distress,” he said.
Kiswahili: Ilipochukuliwa na hatukujua kwa nini, ilituletea dhiki nyingi, "alisema.

English: His family includes 10 children under the age of 18, and his elderly disabled mother, who has diabetes and high blood pressure.
Kiswahili: Familia yake inajumuisha watoto 10 walio na umri wa chini ya miaka 18, na mama yake mzee mlemavu, ambaye ana kisukari na shinikizo la damu.

English: They used to receive 110 kilograms of rice, 40 kilograms of beans, 15 litres of cooking oil, as well as $43 in cash every two months that kept them going.
Kiswahili: Walikuwa wakipokea kilo 110 za mchele, kilo 40 za maharagwe, lita 15 za mafuta ya kupikia, pamoja na pesa taslimu dola 43 kila baada ya miezi miwili ambayo iliwafanya waendelee.

English: He said his family had been assessed last year and classified as being vulnerable and in need of continued assistance.
Kiswahili: Alisema familia yake ilifanyiwa tathmini mwaka jana na kuainishwa kuwa ni dhaifu na inahitaji msaada endelevu.

English: Now, he says, the family survives largely on help from neighbours, who themselves have limited means.
Kiswahili: Sasa, anasema, familia inaishi zaidi kwa msaada kutoka kwa majirani, ambao wenyewe wana uwezo mdogo.

English: Abdullahi works as a night guard, earning about $60 a month.
Kiswahili: Abdullahi anafanya kazi kama mlinzi wa usiku, akipata dola 60 kwa mwezi.

English: He says the income is too little to cover food, basic needs, and the fees for five of his children who were attending Koranic school costing $35 a month.
Kiswahili: Anasema kipato ni kidogo sana kuweza kugharamia chakula, mahitaji ya kimsingi, na ada kwa watoto wake watano waliokuwa wakisoma shule ya Kurani inayogharimu dola 35 kwa mwezi.

English: He has repeatedly visited aid agency offices seeking answers – but often can’t even gain access to the guarded compounds.
Kiswahili: Ametembelea mara kwa mara ofisi za mashirika ya misaada kutafuta majibu - lakini mara nyingi hawezi hata kupata misombo ya ulinzi.

English: We complain about lack of food, but we are told the [aid distribution] card has been removed from the system, and we do not know the reason,” he told Radio Ergo.
Kiswahili: Tunalalamika kuhusu ukosefu wa chakula, lakini tunaambiwa kadi [ya usambazaji wa misaada] imeondolewa kwenye mfumo, na hatujui sababu,” aliiambia Radio Ergo.

English: Abdullahi fled conflict in 1994 in rural Lower Juba region in southern Somalia, where his family had depended on livestock that they have lost.
Kiswahili: Abdullahi alikimbia vita mwaka 1994 katika eneo la vijijini la Juba ya Chini kusini mwa Somalia, ambapo familia yake ilikuwa ikitegemea mifugo ambayo imepoteza.

English: Kenya’s Department of Refugee Services (DRS) removed about 39,000 people from entitlement to assistance in February, following a verification exercise.
Kiswahili: Idara ya Huduma kwa Wakimbizi ya Kenya (DRS) iliwaondoa takriban watu 39,000 kutoka kwa haki ya kupata usaidizi mwezi Februari, kufuatia zoezi la uhakiki.

English: Full details of those removed and the reasons were not available for this report.
Kiswahili: Maelezo kamili ya walioondolewa na sababu hazikupatikana kwa ripoti hii.

English: Camp leaders say some of those removed were reclassified by DRS as Kenyan citizens.
Kiswahili: Viongozi wa kambi hiyo wanasema baadhi ya walioondolewa waliwekwa bayana na DRS kuwa raia wa Kenya.

English: Many of those affected had lived in the camps for more than 30 years.
Kiswahili: Wengi wa walioathiriwa walikuwa wameishi katika kambi hizo kwa zaidi ya miaka 30.

English: Abshiro Adan Ibrahim, 50, applied for citizenship and became a Kenyan citizen in 2025 after living in the Dadaab refugee camps for 32 years.
Kiswahili: Abshiro Adan Ibrahim, 50, aliomba uraia na kuwa raia wa Kenya mnamo 2025 baada ya kuishi katika kambi za wakimbizi za Dadaab kwa miaka 32.

English: She thought this would open better opportunities for her.
Kiswahili: Alifikiri hii ingemfungulia fursa bora zaidi.

English: She continued receiving 100 kilograms of food, 16 litres of cooking oil, and $30 in bi-monthly cash support after her citizenship was granted.
Kiswahili: Aliendelea kupokea kilo 100 za chakula, lita 16 za mafuta ya kupikia, na dola 30 za usaidizi wa kila mwezi wa pesa taslimu baada ya uraia wake kupewa.

English: But in February, her family of eight was on the DRS list of those cut off from assistance.
Kiswahili: Lakini mwezi Februari, familia yake ya watu wanane ilikuwa kwenye orodha ya DRS ya wale waliokatishwa msaada.

English: You can imagine a family of eight losing their food support.
Kiswahili: Unaweza kufikiria familia ya watu wanane ikipoteza msaada wao wa chakula.

English: Neighbours who receive food bring us some, and others bring oil.
Kiswahili: Majirani wanaopokea chakula hutuletea, na wengine huleta mafuta.

English: Sometimes women in the neighbourhood collect about 15 kilos of rice and oil for us,” she said.
Kiswahili: Wakati mwingine wanawake katika ujirani hukusanya takriban kilo 15 za mchele na mafuta kwa ajili yetu,” alisema.

English: Abshiro had hoped that acquiring citizenship would help her find work to support her family, as the aid they received was not enough.
Kiswahili: Abshiro alikuwa na matumaini kwamba kupata uraia kungemsaidia kupata kazi ya kukimu familia yake, kwani msaada waliopokea haukutosha.

English: She now regrets the decision, saying it has left her family without the support they depended on.
Kiswahili: Sasa anajutia uamuzi huo, akisema umeiacha familia yake bila usaidizi walioutegemea.

English: I took that step for my five children who depend on me.
Kiswahili: Nilichukua hatua hiyo kwa watoto wangu watano wanaonitegemea.

English: They have no father and no support.
Kiswahili: Hawana baba na hawana msaada.

English: We cannot manage without refugee assistance,” she told Radio Ergo.
Kiswahili: Hatuwezi kusimamia bila usaidizi wa wakimbizi,” aliiambia Radio Ergo.

English: Abshiro fled insecurity in 1992 in Sanguuni near Kismayo, where her family had relied on farming four hectares of land.
Kiswahili: Abshiro alikimbia ukosefu wa usalama mwaka 1992 huko Sanguuni karibu na Kismayo, ambapo familia yake ilitegemea kilimo cha hekta nne za ardhi.

English: Camp leaders say the impact of refugees being denied aid is severe.
Kiswahili: Viongozi wa kambi wanasema athari za wakimbizi kunyimwa misaada ni kubwa.

English: The chairman of Dadaab’s Hagadera camp, Khalif Dhuubow Jelle, said camp leaders and refugee committees had repeatedly raised their concerns with relevant authorities but to no effect.
Kiswahili: Mwenyekiti wa kambi ya Dadaab ya Hagadera, Khalif Dhuubow Jelle, alisema viongozi wa kambi na kamati za wakimbizi wametoa mara kwa mara wasiwasi wao na mamlaka husika lakini bila matokeo.

English: Life in the camps depends on food and security.
Kiswahili: Maisha katika kambi hutegemea chakula na usalama.

English: If food is removed, there is a serious problem.
Kiswahili: Ikiwa chakula kinaondolewa, kuna shida kubwa.

English: There is hunger, and if assessments are carried out, it will be clear that many are facing it,” Khalif stated.
Kiswahili: Kuna njaa, na ikiwa tathmini itafanywa, itakuwa wazi kuwa wengi wanakabiliwa nayo," Khalif alisema.

English: Refugees in Dadaab currently receive limited water, healthcare, and education services, but continuing food and cash assistance remain central to their survival.
Kiswahili: Wakimbizi katika Dadaab kwa sasa wanapokea huduma chache za maji, afya, na elimu, lakini msaada wa chakula na pesa taslimu unaoendelea unasalia kuwa msingi wa maisha yao.

English: Over the past three years, there has been an incremental reduction in services, food rations and cash support affecting the refugee population in Dadaab.
Kiswahili: Katika kipindi cha miaka mitatu iliyopita, kumekuwa na punguzo la ongezeko la huduma, mgao wa chakula na usaidizi wa fedha unaoathiri idadi ya wakimbizi huko Dadaab.

English: This is attributed to changes in policy around the existence of the camps, as well as to funding cuts.
Kiswahili: Hii inachangiwa na mabadiliko ya sera kuhusu kuwepo kwa kambi, pamoja na kupunguzwa kwa fedha.

English: The Government of Kenya developed a National Anticipatory Action Roadmap with support from partners including the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO),3 which contributed to the co‑development of triggers and thresholds guiding early action.
Kiswahili: Serikali ya Kenya ilibuni Mwongozo wa Kitaifa wa Hatua ya Kutarajia kwa usaidizi kutoka kwa washirika likiwemo Shirika la Chakula na Kilimo la Umoja wa Mataifa (FAO),3 ambao ulichangia katika uundaji wa vichochezi na vizingiti vinavyoelekeza hatua za mapema.

English: Triggers for the nine arid counties were activated based on the standardized precipitation index and vegetation condition index, enabling counties and organizations to commence anticipatory actions.
Kiswahili: Vichochezi vya kaunti tisa zenye ukame viliamilishwa kwa kuzingatia faharasa sanifu ya kunyesha na hali ya uoto, kuwezesha kaunti na mashirika kuanza vitendo vya kutarajia.

English: In response, through SFERA, the Government of Belgium contributed USD 500 000 to FAO to implement a project, entitled “Safeguarding livelihoods and increasing immediate food access for vulnerable rural households affected by forecasted drought through anticipatory action”.
Kiswahili: Katika kukabiliana na hali hiyo, kupitia SFERA, Serikali ya Ubelgiji ilichangia USD 500 000 kwa FAO kutekeleza mradi, unaoitwa "Kulinda riziki na kuongeza upatikanaji wa chakula mara moja kwa kaya za vijijini zilizo hatarini zilizoathiriwa na ukame uliotabiriwa kupitia hatua za kutarajia".

English: Through the project, FAO aims to deliver unconditional cash transfers and enhance access to animal health services for vulnerable households in Garissa, Isiolo, Samburu and Tana River counties.
Kiswahili: Kupitia mradi huo, FAO inalenga kuwasilisha uhamisho wa pesa bila masharti na kuimarisha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya ya wanyama kwa kaya zilizo hatarini katika kaunti za Garissa, Isiolo, Samburu na Tana River.

English: Cash assistance will be provided to enable 2 000 vulnerable families to immediately access food and address urgent basic needs without having to engage in negative coping mechanisms, such as selling animals or withdrawing children from school.
Kiswahili: Usaidizi wa pesa taslimu utatolewa ili kuwezesha familia 2,000 zilizo katika mazingira magumu kupata chakula mara moja na kushughulikia mahitaji ya dharura bila kujihusisha na mbinu mbaya za kukabiliana nazo, kama vile kuuza wanyama au kuwaondoa watoto shuleni.

English: Additionally, 400 000 animals will be dewormed, treated against internal and external parasites and given multivitamins to sustain milk and meat production and survive through this period, directly preserving the livelihoods and nutritional well-being of 4 000 vulnerable households.
Kiswahili: Zaidi ya hayo, wanyama 400,000 watatiwa dawa ya minyoo, watatibiwa dhidi ya vimelea vya ndani na nje na kupewa multivitamini ili kuendeleza uzalishaji wa maziwa na nyama na kuishi katika kipindi hiki, kuhifadhi moja kwa moja maisha na ustawi wa lishe wa kaya 4,000 zilizo katika mazingira magumu.

English: By protecting livestock health and supporting families with cash transfers, FAO and partners will help 6 000 pastoral households survive the drought and preserve their resilience and dignity in the face of disasters and climate shocks.
Kiswahili: Kwa kulinda afya ya mifugo na kusaidia familia kwa uhamisho wa fedha, FAO na washirika watasaidia kaya 6,000 za wafugaji kustahimili ukame na kuhifadhi ustahimilivu na heshima yao katika kukabiliana na majanga na majanga ya hali ya hewa.

English: Breastfeeding is foundational for both child survival and maternal health.
Kiswahili: Kunyonyesha ni msingi wa maisha ya mtoto na afya ya uzazi.

English: It is the first food system and a child’s first vaccine, offering unparalleled nutrition and immune protection.Importantly, breastfeeding is also climate-smart — a renewable, zero-waste, low-carbon act that benefits both people and the planet.
Kiswahili: Ni mfumo wa kwanza wa chakula na chanjo ya kwanza ya mtoto, inayotoa lishe isiyo na kifani na ulinzi wa kinga. Muhimu, kunyonyesha pia kunazingatia hali ya hewa - kitendo kinachoweza kurejeshwa, kisicho na sifuri, cha kaboni ya chini ambacho kinanufaisha watu na sayari.

English: In the fragile and climate-affected context of the Mandera Triangle — where the borders of Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia meet — breastfeeding is a vital lifeline for infants and mothers.
Kiswahili: Katika mazingira dhaifu na yaliyoathiriwa na hali ya hewa ya Pembetatu ya Mandera - ambapo mipaka ya Kenya, Ethiopia, na Somalia inakutana - kunyonyesha ni njia muhimu ya maisha kwa watoto wachanga na akina mama.

English: However, these populations face challenges such as weak health infrastructure, food insecurity, and frequent displacement.
Kiswahili: Hata hivyo, watu hawa wanakabiliwa na changamoto kama vile miundombinu dhaifu ya afya, uhaba wa chakula, na kuhama mara kwa mara.

English: Hanaano which means ‘to nurture’ in Somali is a three-year cross-border nutritionsensitive initiative, supporting women in navigating these challenges, promoting breastfeeding as a culturally rooted and climate-resilient practice.
Kiswahili: Hanaano ambayo ina maana ya 'kulea' katika Kisomali ni mpango wa miaka mitatu wa kuzingatia lishe kuvuka mipaka, kusaidia wanawake katika kukabiliana na changamoto hizi, kuhimiza unyonyeshaji kama desturi iliyokita mizizi na kustahimili hali ya hewa.

English: The Hanaano programme is part of Ireland’s contribution to the Global Action Plan (GAP) on Child Wasting, a collaborative initiative aimed at addressing and reducing child wasting globally.
Kiswahili: Mpango wa Hanaano ni sehemu ya mchango wa Ireland katika Mpango wa Utekelezaji wa Kimataifa (GAP) kuhusu Upotevu wa Mtoto, mpango shirikishi unaolenga kushughulikia na kupunguza upotevu wa watoto duniani kote.

English: Samburu County, Kenya -In Lolmolong village, in Samburu County in northern Kenya, Neshoo Leaungokiok laboured through the night, far from the nearest health facility.
Kiswahili: Kaunti ya Samburu, Kenya -Katika kijiji cha Lolmolong, katika Kaunti ya Samburu kaskazini mwa Kenya, Neshoo Leaungokiok alifanya kazi usiku kucha, mbali na kituo cha afya cha karibu.

English: With no transport available and a six-hour journey on foot to reach care, she had no choice but to give birth at home, assisted only by a traditional birth attendant.
Kiswahili: Kwa kuwa hakukuwa na usafiri na mwendo wa saa sita kwa miguu kufika kwenye huduma, hakuwa na budi ila kujifungulia nyumbani, akisaidiwa na mkunga wa jadi pekee.

English: In this remote part of Kenya, long distances, difficult terrain and insecurity continue to limit access to skilled care during childbirth, placing mothers and newborns at significant risk.
Kiswahili: Katika sehemu hii ya mbali ya Kenya, umbali mrefu, ardhi ngumu na ukosefu wa usalama unaendelea kuzuia upatikanaji wa huduma za kitaalamu wakati wa kujifungua, hivyo kuwaweka akina mama na watoto wachanga katika hatari kubwa.

English: As labour intensified, Lucy Makena, a reproductive health officer from a nearby mobile outreach clinic supported by World Health Organization (WHO), reached the home.
Kiswahili: Uchungu ulipokuwa ukiongezeka, Lucy Makena, afisa wa afya ya uzazi kutoka kliniki inayohamishika ya karibu inayoungwa mkono na Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO), alifika nyumbani hapo.

English: These clinics operate from off-road trucks fitted with a consultation room, patient beds, a small laboratory, and a pharmacy, designed to bring essential care directly to communities far from any permanent facility.
Kiswahili: Kliniki hizi zinafanya kazi kutoka kwa malori ya nje ya barabara yaliyo na chumba cha mashauriano, vitanda vya wagonjwa, maabara ndogo, na duka la dawa, iliyoundwa kuleta huduma muhimu moja kwa moja kwa jamii zilizo mbali na kituo chochote cha kudumu.

English: Trained in emergency obstetric and newborn care through programmes also supported by WHO, she quickly assessed Leaungokiok’s condition and began monitoring the progress of labour.
Kiswahili: Akiwa amefunzwa katika utunzaji wa dharura wa uzazi na watoto wachanga kupitia programu zinazoungwa mkono na WHO, alitathmini haraka hali ya Leaungokiok na kuanza kufuatilia maendeleo ya leba.

English: Makena’s presence came at a critical moment.
Kiswahili: Uwepo wa Makena ulikuja wakati muhimu.

English: In settings where complications can go unrecognized and timely care is often out of reach, skilled support can mean the difference between life and death.
Kiswahili: Katika mazingira ambapo matatizo yanaweza kwenda bila kutambuliwa na huduma ya wakati mara nyingi haipatikani, usaidizi wenye ujuzi unaweza kumaanisha tofauti kati ya maisha na kifo.

English: With Makena’s support and care, Leaungokiok safely delivered a healthy baby girl.
Kiswahili: Kwa usaidizi na utunzaji wa Makena, Leaungokiok alijifungua salama mtoto wa kike mwenye afya njema.

English: Shortly after birth, however, Leaungokiok began to bleed heavily, a potentially life-threatening complication if not managed quickly.
Kiswahili: Hata hivyo, muda mfupi baada ya kuzaliwa, Leaungokiok alianza kuvuja damu nyingi, tatizo ambalo lingeweza kutishia maisha lisipodhibitiwa haraka.

English: Acting immediately, Lucy administered lifesaving medicine to control the bleeding.
Kiswahili: Kuchukua hatua mara moja, Lucy alitoa dawa ya kuokoa maisha ili kudhibiti damu.

English: She also supported the mother to initiate breastfeeding within the first hour, an essential step in protecting the newborn’s health.
Kiswahili: Pia alimsaidia mama kuanza kunyonyesha ndani ya saa ya kwanza, hatua muhimu katika kulinda afya ya mtoto mchanga.

English: I am grateful to have delivered safely and to have a healthy baby,” says Leaungokiok, cradling her daughter.
Kiswahili: Ninashukuru kujifungua salama na kupata mtoto mwenye afya njema,” anasema Leaungokiok, akimkumbatia bintiye.

English: For Makena, moments like this reflect the importance of reaching women wherever they are.
Kiswahili: Kwa Makena, nyakati kama hizi zinaonyesha umuhimu wa kuwafikia wanawake popote walipo.

English: Through collaboration with county health authorities, WHO is supporting the expansion of emergency obstetric and newborn care services in northern Kenya, including training health workers and strengthening outreach services to reach remote communities.
Kiswahili: Kupitia ushirikiano na mamlaka za afya za kaunti, WHO inaunga mkono upanuzi wa huduma za dharura za uzazi na watoto wachanga kaskazini mwa Kenya, ikiwa ni pamoja na kutoa mafunzo kwa wahudumu wa afya na kuimarisha huduma za kufikia jamii za mbali.

English: While access to skilled birth care remains limited in some parts of the African Region, targeted investments in health workforce training and service delivery are helping to close the gap, as in Leaungokiok’s case.
Kiswahili: Ingawa upatikanaji wa huduma ya uzazi yenye ujuzi bado ni mdogo katika baadhi ya maeneo ya Kanda ya Afrika, uwekezaji unaolengwa katika mafunzo ya wafanyakazi wa afya na utoaji wa huduma unasaidia kuziba pengo hilo, kama ilivyo kwa Leaungokiok.

English: As 2025 unfolded amid growing regional uncertainty and humanitarian challenges, the ICRC Nairobi Regional Delegation continued to work alongside partners and communities to uphold humanity where it was needed most.
Kiswahili: Mwaka wa 2025 ulipoendelea huku kukiwa na hali ya kutokuwa na uhakika na changamoto za kibinadamu zinazoongezeka kikanda, Ujumbe wa ICRC wa Mkoa wa Nairobi uliendelea kufanya kazi pamoja na washirika na jamii ili kudumisha ubinadamu mahali palipohitajika zaidi.

English: Through humanitarian diplomacy, engagement, and operational support, our efforts remained focused on protecting dignity, strengthening respect for International Humanitarian Law, and responding to the evolving needs of people affected by conflict and violence across the region.
Kiswahili: Kupitia diplomasia ya kibinadamu, ushirikishwaji, na usaidizi wa kiutendaji, juhudi zetu zilibaki zikilenga katika kulinda utu, kuimarisha heshima kwa Sheria ya Kimataifa ya Kibinadamu, na kujibu mahitaji yanayoendelea ya watu walioathiriwa na migogoro na vurugu katika eneo lote.

English: While Kenya demonstrates sustained steady economic growth, the gains remain uneven.
Kiswahili: Huku Kenya ikionyesha ukuaji endelevu wa uchumi, mafanikio bado hayafanani.

English: Poverty, inequality and regional disparities persist, especially in arid and semi-arid counties, which cover 80% of the country’s land mass.
Kiswahili: Umaskini, ukosefu wa usawa na tofauti za kikanda zinaendelea, haswa katika kaunti kame na nusu kame, ambazo huchukua 80% ya ardhi yote ya nchi.

English: Societal gaps continue to limit the full participation of women, and other marginalised groups, and they suffer greater hurdles to recover from crises, including exclusion from assistance, especially material assistance.
Kiswahili: Mapengo ya kijamii yanaendelea kupunguza ushiriki kamili wa wanawake, na makundi mengine yaliyotengwa, na wanapata vikwazo vikubwa vya kujikwamua kutokana na matatizo, ikiwa ni pamoja na kutengwa na usaidizi, hasa msaada wa nyenzo.

English: Climate shocks, including recurring droughts and floods, are intensifying existing vulnerabilities and eroding household resilience.
Kiswahili: Matukio ya hali ya hewa, ikiwa ni pamoja na ukame na mafuriko ya mara kwa mara, yanazidisha udhaifu uliopo na kumomonyoa ustahimilivu wa kaya.

English: In times of crisis, communities in need of support require assistance that is versatile to suit their needs and support recovery.
Kiswahili: Wakati wa shida, jamii zinazohitaji usaidizi zinahitaji usaidizi ambao unaweza kukidhi mahitaji yao na kusaidia ahueni.

English: In this context, the Kenya Cash Consortium (KCC) plays a critical role in supporting crisis-affected communities to meet their most urgent needs, as they define and prioritise them, in a flexible, dignified manner.
Kiswahili: Katika muktadha huu, Muungano wa Fedha wa Kenya (KCC) una jukumu muhimu katika kusaidia jamii zilizoathiriwa na mgogoro ili kukidhi mahitaji yao ya dharura, huku wakiyafafanua na kuyapa kipaumbele, kwa njia inayoweza kunyumbulika na yenye heshima.

English: Since 2019, the KCC has been continuously providing timely, life-saving cash assistance to communities across Kenya.
Kiswahili: Tangu 2019, KCC imekuwa ikitoa usaidizi wa pesa kwa wakati unaofaa kwa jamii kote nchini Kenya.

English: Led by Acted in partnership with the ASAL Humanitarian Network, Oxfam Kenya, Concern Worldwide and IMPACT Initiatives, the KCC’s objective is to deliver timely cash assistance to communities affected by various shocks including natural disasters, conflict and displacement.
Kiswahili: Ikiongozwa na Kutenda kwa ushirikiano na Mtandao wa Kibinadamu wa ASAL, Oxfam Kenya, Concern Worldwide na IMPACT Initiatives, lengo la KCC ni kutoa usaidizi wa pesa taslimu kwa wakati kwa jamii zilizoathiriwa na majanga mbalimbali ikiwa ni pamoja na majanga ya asili, migogoro na kuhama makazi yao.

English: Over the years, the KCC has developed tools, systems and mechanisms to provide targeted and timely multi-sectoral responses based on needs prioritisation and continuous market monitoring, enabling the consortium to offer assistance to over 500,000 individuals.
Kiswahili: Kwa miaka mingi, KCC imeunda zana, mifumo na mbinu za kutoa majibu yaliyolengwa na kwa wakati mwafaka ya sekta mbalimbali kulingana na kipaumbele cha mahitaji na ufuatiliaji endelevu wa soko, kuwezesha muungano huo kutoa usaidizi kwa zaidi ya watu 500,000.

English: The KCC works through a locally-led approach with more than 30 local NGOs engaged in response delivery and county-level monitoring, data collection and coordination with county governments.
Kiswahili: KCC inafanya kazi kupitia mkabala unaoongozwa na wenyeji na zaidi ya NGOs 30 za ndani zinazojishughulisha na utoaji wa majibu na ufuatiliaji wa ngazi ya kaunti, ukusanyaji wa data na uratibu na serikali za kaunti.

English: The KCC’s approach is guided by the understanding that how assistance is delivered, the scale of delivery, and what partners are engaged, account for the largest gains and losses in efficiency and effectiveness.
Kiswahili: Mtazamo wa KCC unaongozwa na ufahamu kwamba jinsi usaidizi unavyotolewa, ukubwa wa utoaji, na washirika gani wanashirikishwa, huchangia faida na hasara kubwa zaidi katika ufanisi na ufanisi.

English: In 2026, the KCC, with support from the European Commission (DG ECHO), is implementing an action focused on providing Multipurpose Cash Assistance (MPCA) to crisis–affected communities across Kenya.
Kiswahili: Mnamo 2026, KCC, kwa usaidizi wa Tume ya Ulaya (DG ECHO), inatekeleza hatua inayolenga kutoa Msaada wa Pesa Madhumuni (MPCA) kwa jamii zilizoathiriwa na shida kote nchini Kenya.

English: Under the 9-month intervention, the KCC aims to reach over 28,000 individuals across the country.
Kiswahili: Chini ya uingiliaji kati wa miezi 9, KCC inalenga kufikia zaidi ya watu 28,000 kote nchini.

English: This includes communities across Kenya’s Arid and Semi-Arid lands affected by increasingly recurrent climate-related crises such as drought and flooding.
Kiswahili: Hii ni pamoja na jamii katika nchi Kame na Nusu Kame nchini Kenya zilizoathiriwa na majanga yanayohusiana na hali ya hewa yanayotokea mara kwa mara kama vile ukame na mafuriko.

English: Other key beneficiaries are refugees who, despite receiving protection and nutrition assistance, are at risk of falling further into vulnerability.
Kiswahili: Walengwa wengine wakuu ni wakimbizi ambao, licha ya kupokea msaada wa ulinzi na lishe, wako katika hatari ya kuangukia zaidi katika mazingira magumu.

English: These groups are referred through key protection and referral actors in Kenya to receive MPCA as an additional layer to support recovery.
Kiswahili: Makundi haya yanarejelewa kupitia wahusika wakuu wa ulinzi na rufaa nchini Kenya ili kupokea MPCA kama safu ya ziada ya kusaidia uokoaji.

English: This approach and the collaboration established between sectoral leaders also positions the KCC as a leader in adopting ‘MPCA first’ as a guiding principle to increase value for recipients.
Kiswahili: Mbinu hii na ushirikiano ulioanzishwa kati ya viongozi wa kisekta pia unaweka KCC kama kiongozi katika kupitisha ‘MPCA kwanza’ kama kanuni elekezi ya kuongeza thamani kwa wapokeaji.

English: Further, the KCC will continue critical work in disaster-preparedness in Kenya, leading on county-level and national-level stakeholder engagement to advocate for concrete policy and action to protect Kenyan communities from recurring or predictable crises.
Kiswahili: Zaidi ya hayo, KCC itaendelea na kazi muhimu ya kujiandaa na maafa nchini Kenya, ikiongoza katika ushirikishwaji wa washikadau katika ngazi ya kaunti na kitaifa ili kutetea sera na hatua madhubuti za kulinda jamii za Kenya dhidi ya majanga ya mara kwa mara au yanayotabirika.

English: Our aim under this action is not just to transfer resources; but to transfer choice, dignity and hope.
Kiswahili: Lengo letu chini ya hatua hii sio tu kuhamisha rasilimali; bali kuhamisha chaguo, heshima na matumaini.

English: Abdirizak Kontoma, Kenya Cash Consortium Coordinator and Project Manager, on the goals of the 2026 action.
Kiswahili: Abdirizak Kontoma, Mratibu wa Kenya Cash Consortium na Meneja Mradi, kuhusu malengo ya hatua ya 2026.

English: Through this action, the KCC aims to reinforce its role as a provider of flexible, efficient humanitarian assistance, leveraging strong partnerships and coordination to maximise impact, strengthen resilience, and advance a more coherent and forward-looking response to recurrent crises in Kenya.
Kiswahili: Kupitia hatua hii, KCC inalenga kuimarisha jukumu lake kama mtoaji wa usaidizi wa kibinadamu unaobadilika na unaofaa, kutumia ushirikiano dhabiti na uratibu ili kuongeza athari, kuimarisha uthabiti, na kuendeleza mwitikio thabiti na unaotazamia mbele zaidi kwa mizozo ya mara kwa mara nchini Kenya.

English: In 2023, catalytic support from Google.org launched a groundbreaking partnership between WFP, the University of Oxford, and key regional institutions—IGAD’s Climate Prediction and Applications Centre (ICPAC), the Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD), and the Ethiopia Meteorological Institute (EMI).
Kiswahili: Mnamo 2023, usaidizi wa kichocheo kutoka Google.org ulizindua ushirikiano wa msingi kati ya WFP, Chuo Kikuu cha Oxford, na taasisi muhimu za kikanda-IGAD's Climate Prediction and Application Center (ICPAC), Idara ya Hali ya Hewa ya Kenya (KMD), na Taasisi ya Hali ya Hewa ya Ethiopia (EMI).

English: The goal: to harness machine learning to improve early warning systems for extreme weather in eastern Africa.
Kiswahili: Lengo: kutumia ujifunzaji wa mashine ili kuboresha mifumo ya tahadhari ya mapema kwa hali mbaya ya hewa katika Afrika mashariki.

English: Through the Strengthening Early Warning Systems 1 A Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (CGAN) for climate forecasting is an advanced type of artificial intelligence that helps predict weather patterns by learning from past climate data.
Kiswahili: Kupitia Mifumo ya Kuimarisha Tahadhari ya Mapema 1 Mtandao wa Masharti ya Kuzalisha Masharti (CGAN) kwa utabiri wa hali ya hewa ni aina ya hali ya juu ya akili ya bandia ambayo husaidia kutabiri mifumo ya hali ya hewa kwa kujifunza kutoka kwa data ya zamani ya hali ya hewa.

English: CGAN takes these specific conditions—like temperature, humidity, or wind patterns—and uses them to generate realistic future climate scenarios.
Kiswahili: CGAN inachukua hali hizi mahususi—kama vile halijoto, unyevunyevu, au mifumo ya upepo—na kuzitumia kuzalisha hali halisi za hali ya hewa za siku zijazo.

English: This helps scientists and meteorologists make more accurate forecasts, especially in complex situations like predicting droughts or floods for Anticipatory Action (SEWAA) project, the team is applying advanced AI—specifically Conditional Generative Adversarial Networks (cGANs)1 —to generate high-resolution rainfall forecasts weeks in advance.
Kiswahili: Hii huwasaidia wanasayansi na wataalamu wa hali ya hewa kufanya utabiri sahihi zaidi, hasa katika hali ngumu kama vile kutabiri ukame au mafuriko kwa mradi wa Anticipatory Action (SEWAA), timu inatumia AI ya hali ya juu—hasa Mitandao ya Masharti ya Kuzalisha Masharti (cGANs)1—kutoa utabiri wa mvua wa msongo wa juu wiki mapema.

English: This innovation empowers national meteorological services to produce accurate, timely forecasts using only a personal computer—eliminating the need for costly supercomputers and unlocking new potential for anticipatory action in climate-vulnerable regions.
Kiswahili: Ubunifu huu huwezesha huduma za kitaifa za hali ya hewa kutoa utabiri sahihi, kwa wakati ufaao kwa kutumia kompyuta ya kibinafsi pekee—kuondoa hitaji la kompyuta kubwa za gharama kubwa na kufungua uwezekano mpya wa hatua za kutazamia katika maeneo yenye mazingira magumu ya hali ya hewa.

English: UNAIDS uses ‘cost of inaction’ as an indicator of the negative impact on the lives of people and communities of not investing resources to end AIDS.
Kiswahili: UNAIDS inatumia ‘gharama ya kutochukua hatua’ kama kiashirio cha athari mbaya kwa maisha ya watu na jamii ya kutowekeza rasilimali kukomesha UKIMWI.

English: This issue is particularly significant with regard to the provision of HIV and sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) for young people in eastern and southern Africa.
Kiswahili: Suala hili ni muhimu hasa kuhusiana na utoaji wa VVU na afya ya ngono na uzazi na haki (SRHR) kwa vijana mashariki na kusini mwa Afrika.

English: The cost of inaction for young people is especially significant as the impact extends through most of their life.
Kiswahili: Gharama ya kutochukua hatua kwa vijana ni muhimu sana kwani athari huenea katika sehemu kubwa ya maisha yao.

English: This report explores the costs of inaction in not realising the HIV and SRHR needs of young people in Kenya, Rwanda and Uganda, focusing on costs related to: 1) adolescent pregnancies, 2) HIV acquisition and 3) gender-based violation.
Kiswahili: Ripoti hii inachunguza gharama za kutochukua hatua katika kutotambua mahitaji ya VVU na SRHR ya vijana nchini Kenya, Rwanda na Uganda, ikizingatia gharama zinazohusiana na: 1) mimba za utotoni, 2) kupata VVU na 3) ukiukaji wa kijinsia.

English: Using a cost of inaction approach, this report calculates the annual current cost of the inadequate provision of HIV and SRHR services to young people aged 15–24 years old in the three countries.
Kiswahili: Kwa kutumia mbinu ya gharama ya kutochukua hatua, ripoti hii inakokotoa gharama ya sasa ya kila mwaka ya utoaji duni wa huduma za VVU na SRHR kwa vijana wenye umri wa miaka 15-24 katika nchi hizo tatu.

English: This provides a foundation for alternative policies to be costed to address these gaps and a true cost–benefit analysis to be conducted.
Kiswahili: Hili linatoa msingi kwa sera mbadala kugharamiwa kushughulikia mapengo haya na uchanganuzi wa kweli wa gharama na manufaa utakaofanywa.

English: NAIROBI, Kenya— Kenya is entering a decisive moment in its response to forced displacement.
Kiswahili: NAIROBI, Kenya- Kenya inaingia katika wakati madhubuti katika kukabiliana na kulazimishwa kuhama makazi yao.

English: As the country shifts from an encampment-based model toward integrated settlements under the Shirika Plan, new evidence points to a central risk:without jobs, the current drop in aid is translating into deeper poverty rather than self‑reliance for both host communities and refugees.
Kiswahili: Wakati nchi inapohama kutoka katika mfumo wa msingi wa kambi kuelekea kwenye makazi jumuishi chini ya Mpango wa Shiŕika, ushahidi mpya unaonyesha hatari kuu: bila kazi, kushuka kwa sasa kwa misaada kunasababisha umaskini mkubwa badala ya kujitegemea kwa jumuiya zinazowapokea na wakimbizi.

English: A recent studyproduced through a collaboration between the World Bank, UNHCR, and the Center for Effective Global Action draws on two waves of a panel survey conducted between 2022 and 2024.
Kiswahili: Utafiti wa hivi majuzi uliotolewa kupitia ushirikiano kati ya Benki ya Dunia, UNHCR, na Kituo cha Matendo Mazuri ya Ulimwenguni unategemea mawimbi mawili ya uchunguzi wa jopo uliofanywa kati ya 2022 na 2024.

English: By following the same refugee and host households over time, the study provides rare insight into how welfare and coping strategies are evolving as humanitarian aid tightens, shocks intensify, and local economies come under strain.
Kiswahili: Kwa kufuata wakimbizi na kaya zinazowahifadhi kwa muda, utafiti unatoa mwangaza adimu wa jinsi mikakati ya ustawi na kukabiliana na hali inavyoendelea huku misaada ya kibinadamu inavyozidi kuwa ngumu, mishtuko inazidi, na uchumi wa ndani unakabiliwa na matatizo.

English: As global aid budgets shrink, refugees and host communities are increasingly competing in the same thin markets and relying on the same under‑resourced schools, clinics, roads, and water systems, raising the stakes for local livelihoods.
Kiswahili: Kadiri bajeti za misaada ya kimataifa zinavyopungua, wakimbizi na jumuiya zinazowapokea wanazidi kushindana katika masoko nyembamba sawa na kutegemea shule zisizo na rasilimali, zahanati, barabara na mifumo ya maji, hivyo basi kuinua hali ya maisha ya wenyeji.

English: Forced displacement is both a humanitarian issue and, increasingly, a challenge for jobs and development, with direct implications for poverty reduction, local economic stability, and social cohesion.
Kiswahili: Uhamisho wa kulazimishwa ni suala la kibinadamu na, inazidi kuwa changamoto kwa kazi na maendeleo, yenye athari za moja kwa moja katika kupunguza umaskini, utulivu wa kiuchumi wa ndani, na uwiano wa kijamii.

English: Because the surveys predate the deepest aid cuts observed since late 2024, conditions today are likely even more severe.
Kiswahili: Kwa sababu tafiti zilitangulia upunguzaji mkubwa wa misaada ulioonekana tangu mwishoni mwa 2024, hali leo huenda ni mbaya zaidi.

English: Poverty and deprivation remain widespread among both host communities and refugees, with little improvement over time.
Kiswahili: Umaskini na kunyimwa bado kumeenea miongoni mwa jumuiya zinazowapokea na wakimbizi, na uboreshaji mdogo kadri muda unavyopita.

English: More than four out of five refugees in Kakuma, Kalobeyei, and Dadaab are multidimensionally poor, experiencing overlapping deprivations in education, employment, housing, water, and nutrition.
Kiswahili: Zaidi ya wakimbizi wanne kati ya watano huko Kakuma, Kalobeyei, na Dadaab ni masikini wa pande nyingi, wanakabiliwa na kunyimwa kwa elimu, ajira, makazi, maji na lishe.

English: Host communities, particularly in Turkana, face similar hardship, reflecting longstanding development gaps.
Kiswahili: Jumuiya mwenyeji, haswa Turkana, zinakabiliwa na ugumu kama huo, unaoonyesha mapungufu ya muda mrefu ya maendeleo.

English: Between survey waves, the median value of aid received by refugee households fell following repeated ration cuts and the suspension of voucher systems.
Kiswahili: Kati ya mawimbi ya uchunguzi, thamani ya wastani ya misaada iliyopokelewa na kaya za wakimbizi ilishuka kufuatia kupunguzwa kwa mgao mara kwa mara na kusimamishwa kwa mifumo ya vocha.

English: Because aid still accounted for about three quarters of total revenue for camp‑based refugees in 2024, these cuts translated directly into falling household incomes and weaker demand in local camp economies, squeezing host community livelihoods tied to trade, services, and casual work.
Kiswahili: Kwa sababu misaada bado ilichangia takriban robo tatu ya mapato ya jumla ya wakimbizi waliokaa kambini mwaka wa 2024, upunguzaji huu ulitafsiri moja kwa moja katika kupungua kwa mapato ya kaya na mahitaji duni katika uchumi wa kambi za ndani, kubana riziki za jamii zinazohusishwa na biashara, huduma, na kazi za kawaida.

English: This is most evident in Kalobeyei, where monetary poverty rose markedly between survey waves.
Kiswahili: Hili linadhihirika zaidi Kalobeyei, ambako umaskini wa kifedha uliongezeka sana kati ya mawimbi ya utafiti.

English: Refugee labor markets deteriorated sharply amid already weak host employment conditions.
Kiswahili: Masoko ya ajira ya wakimbizi yalizorota kwa kasi huku kukiwa na hali dhaifu ya ajira ya wenyeji.

English: Refugee employment halved between survey waves, falling from 13% to 7% in camps and from 50% to 37% in urban areas.
Kiswahili: Ajira kwa wakimbizi ilipungua kwa nusu kati ya mawimbi ya utafiti, ikishuka kutoka 13% hadi 7% katika kambi na kutoka 50% hadi 37% katika maeneo ya mijini.

English: Inactivity among camp‑based refugees rose to 89%, reflecting limited job opportunities rather than unwillingness to work, with 64% citing a lack of available jobs, up from 41%.
Kiswahili: Ukosefu wa shughuli miongoni mwa wakimbizi walio katika kambi uliongezeka hadi 89%, ikionyesha nafasi finyu za kazi badala ya kutokuwa tayari kufanya kazi, huku 64% wakitaja ukosefu wa kazi zilizopo, kutoka 41%.

English: Host employment changed little but remained low, especially in camp‑adjacent areas where employment edged down from 35% to 33% and inactivity rose to 63%.
Kiswahili: Ajira ya wakaribishaji ilibadilika kidogo lakini ilibaki chini, haswa katika maeneo ya karibu na kambi ambapo ajira ilipungua kutoka 35% hadi 33% na kutofanya kazi kulipanda hadi 63%.

English: Overall, the results point to a sharp refugee labor market shock alongside limited absorptive capacity in host labor markets.
Kiswahili: Kwa ujumla, matokeo yanaonyesha mshtuko mkali wa soko la ajira la wakimbizi sambamba na uwezo mdogo wa kufyonza katika soko la ajira mwenyeji.

English: Structural barriers further constrain access to work.
Kiswahili: Vizuizi vya kimuundo vinazuia zaidi ufikiaji wa kazi.

English: In the first wave of the survey, less than 1% of refugees held a work permit, underscoring how administrative hurdles and mobility restrictions continue to constrain formal labor‑market entry.
Kiswahili: Katika wimbi la kwanza la utafiti, chini ya 1% ya wakimbizi walikuwa na kibali cha kufanya kazi, ikisisitiza jinsi vikwazo vya kiutawala na vizuizi vya uhamaji vinaendelea kuwabana kuingia rasmi kwenye soko la kazi.

English: Host communities face a parallel challenge: chronically weak labor demand in Turkana and Garissa, compounded by shrinking aid that further contracts service‑oriented camp economies.
Kiswahili: Jumuiya mwenyeji zinakabiliwa na changamoto sambamba: mahitaji dhaifu ya wafanyikazi huko Turkana na Garissa, yakichangiwa na kupungua kwa misaada ambayo inaweka kandarasi zaidi uchumi wa kambi unaozingatia huduma.

English: Low female labor participation in camps reflects the intersection of care responsibilities, limited mobility, and weak job availability; host‑community women in lagging regions face similar barriers.
Kiswahili: Ushiriki mdogo wa kazi ya wanawake katika kambi huonyesha makutano ya majukumu ya utunzaji, uhamaji mdogo, na upatikanaji dhaifu wa kazi; wanawake wa jamii-wageni katika maeneo yaliyochelewa wanakabiliwa na vikwazo sawa.

English: Without deliberate investments in childcare, skills, and safe work opportunities, women risk exclusion from the gains of integration.
Kiswahili: Bila uwekezaji wa kimakusudi katika malezi ya watoto, ujuzi, na fursa salama za kazi, wanawake wanahatarisha kutengwa kutoka kwa mafanikio ya ujumuishaji.

English: Food prices also remain a persistent stressor, while excessive rainfall and flooding in 2024 disrupted livelihoods and damaged infrastructure, particularly affecting host communities reliant on agriculture and livestock.
Kiswahili: Bei ya vyakula pia inasalia kuwa mkazo unaoendelea, wakati mvua nyingi na mafuriko katika 2024 yalitatiza maisha na miundombinu iliyoharibiwa, haswa kuathiri jamii zinazotegemea kilimo na mifugo.

English: As incomes fall and food becomes less affordable, households increasingly resort to negative coping strategies, signaling eroding resilience.
Kiswahili: Kadiri mapato yanavyopungua na chakula kinapokuwa chini ya bei nafuu, kaya zinazidi kutumia mikakati mibaya ya kukabiliana na hali hiyo, kuashiria kumomonyoka kwa ustahimilivu.

English: In camp settings, 64% of households reported reducing food consumption and 44% relied on credit to buy food.
Kiswahili: Katika mazingira ya kambi, 64% ya kaya ziliripoti kupunguza matumizi ya chakula na 44% zilitegemea mkopo kununua chakula.

English: Children are bearing the brunt:complementary evidencepoints to worsening nutrition outcomes, and suggest sustained nutritional stress rather than short‑term shocks alone.
Kiswahili: Watoto wanabeba mzigo huu:ushahidi wa nyongeza wa matokeo mabaya ya lishe, na kupendekeza dhiki endelevu ya lishe badala ya mishtuko ya muda mfupi pekee.

English: Gaps in nutrition service coverage further compound these risks.
Kiswahili: Mapungufu katika utoaji wa huduma ya lishe huongeza zaidi hatari hizi.
